name: The Waking Crown
description: A newly crowned monarch must steer a kingdom where slumbering magic stirs, wards crumble, a dragon circles, and the mage guild sharpens its ambitions.

cards.prompt: On coronation night the palace wards flicker and gutter like dying candles. Your steward whispers that the runes carved by your ancestors are failing after three centuries. Do you investigate the old crypts yourself, or summon the court mages to explain?

rightChoice.text: Summon the mage guild to court

cards.prompt: The Archmagus arrives uninvited and declares the guild will no longer answer to the throne. 'The old magic answers to us, not to crowns,' she says. The court holds its breath.

rightChoice.text: Grant limited autonomy in exchange for aid

cards.prompt: A shepherd gallops in, ashen: a dragon has been sighted wheeling beyond the northern peaks, the first in living memory. Panic ripples through the market square.

leftChoice.text: Muster the border garrisons at once

rightChoice.text: Send scholars to study it before acting

cards.prompt: The autumn harvest rites approach. The elder priestess admits the crown has half-forgotten the words that bless the fields. Without them, folk fear the crop will rot in the earth.

leftChoice.text: Reconstruct the rites from crypt texts

rightChoice.text: Let the village hedge-witches lead them

cards.prompt: With the guild empowered, a faction of nobles accuses you of surrendering the realm to sorcerers. They gather in a shadowed hall, plotting.

rightChoice.text: Invite them to help govern the mages

cards.prompt: A ward-stone at the eastern gate shatters, and a pack of shadow-beasts slips through in the night. Guards die. The people demand protection now.

leftChoice.text: Spend the treasury reforging the wards

rightChoice.text: Bind the beasts with forbidden pacts

cards.prompt: A crowd of villagers arrives bearing charms and salt, begging you to hang iron over every doorway as their grandmothers did. The court scholars scoff at the superstition.

rightChoice.text: Ban the charms as ignorant panic

cards.prompt: An envoy claims the dragon can speak, and it demands tribute in gold and cattle to spare your northern towns. Your generals bristle at the insult.

leftChoice.text: Pay the tribute to buy time

cards.prompt: The mage guild offers to teach you a single true spell of your choosing, but every lesson leaves you changed, colder, hungry for more power. Your advisor warns of addiction.

rightChoice.text: Decline and keep your mind your own

cards.prompt: A ragged hedge-witch is dragged before you, accused of stealing crown grimoires. She claims she was only saving knowledge the guild would have burned.

leftChoice.text: Side with the guild and punish her

rightChoice.text: Pardon her and take her into service

cards.prompt: Scouts return breathless: the dragon has begun burning the border villages. Refugees stream toward the capital, and the treasury groans under their needs.

leftChoice.text: Open the granaries to the refugees

rightChoice.text: Seal the gates to conserve supplies

cards.prompt: A rite of binding could shackle the waking magic to the crown forever, but it requires a sacrifice of arcane lore, burning ancient tomes to fuel it. The gain would be certain, the loss permanent.

leftChoice.text: Burn the tomes to seize control

rightChoice.text: Preserve the knowledge, risk the chaos

cards.prompt: Your treasurer proposes a grand tax on the guild's enchanted goods to refill the coffers. The mages threaten to leave the kingdom entirely if you dare.

rightChoice.text: Exempt the guild to keep them close

cards.prompt: The reconstructed harvest rite works, and the fields blaze golden. But the ceremony has awakened something beneath the old barrow, and the ground trembles at dusk.

leftChoice.text: Seal the barrow with the guild's aid

rightChoice.text: Open it and claim what sleeps within

cards.prompt: The dragon lands upon the palace walls, wings blotting the sun. It offers a bargain: bind your bloodline to its ancient service, and it will defend the realm for a hundred years.

rightChoice.text: Fight it with everything you have

cards.prompt: Two claimants to the guild's leadership come before you: a cautious traditionalist and a bold reformer who would share magic with commoners. The realm's future in sorcery hangs on your choice.
The waking magic reaches its crest. The wards, the dragon, the guild, the rites, all bend toward a single reckoning. Your council begs you to choose how the crown will meet the new age.

leftChoice.text: Rule as a sorcerer-monarch, magic and crown as one

rightChoice.text: Return magic to the people and rule humbly

cards.prompt: On the eve of the great reckoning, an assassin's blade or a whispered spell, none can tell which, nearly ends your reign. You survive, shaken, and must decide your final stance before the dawn.

leftChoice.text: Root out all enemies with iron resolve

rightChoice.text: Forgive and unite the fractured court

cards.prompt: Dawn breaks over a transformed kingdom. The old magic no longer sleeps, yet neither does it consume you. Your name is spoken in the same breath as the founders. History will remember the choices that made this age. How shall the chronicles record your reign?

leftChoice.text: As the monarch who tamed the waking world

rightChoice.text: As the ruler who set the old magic free
